pr.title: Update billing copy
pr.description: Refresh the Free plan copy on the Billing page so it's clearer about what upgrading unlocks.

visual_comparisons.summary: No changes detected between the baseline and the PR version.
The settings view has been redesigned from a dedicated full-page view to a modal dialog overlay on the dashboard. Additionally, the 'Dark mode' toggle option is missing in the new modal version.
The subtext under the Billing header was updated with new copy describing the benefits of upgrading.

in_scope: Updating the Free plan description text on the Billing page (billing.html) to clarify what upgrading unlocks.
out_of_scope: Refactoring the Settings button in index.html from an anchor link (settings.html) to a button element.
Adding a settings modal component (HTML markup and inline CSS classes) to index.html.
Adding JavaScript functions (openSettings and closeSettings) to index.html to manage modal state.
scope_analysis.reasoning: The PR includes a major, undocumented behavioral change. While the billing copy update is in-scope, the modification to index.html entirely changes how the Settings feature works (converting a page navigation into a modal). This represents severe scope creep and must be rejected and split into a separate PR for proper QA and tracking.

code_review.summary: The pull request contains significant changes to the homepage (index.html), converting the Settings link into an in-page modal. This behavioral and structural change is completely outside the stated scope of updating the billing copy on the billing page.
concerns: Changes in index.html are entirely unrelated to the stated scope of updating the billing copy on billing.html.
Replaces standard page navigation to settings.html with an in-page modal, which might break bookmarks, direct links, or SEO, and leaves settings.html potentially orphaned.
Introduces new UI elements, inline Javascript event handlers, and a modal backdrop that were not declared, reviewed, or tested for this task.
